Items.Title: Lodash Concat 和 Javascript Native Concat 的区别 | Difference between Lodash Concat and Javascript Native Concat
Items.Description:
它不会改变原始数组。

它易于使用且简单。 但是这两种方法有什么区别呢？

很明显，一个是使用库（Lodash），另一个是 Javascript 原始连接。

语法也不同。 但是，本机 Javascript concat 获胜。 我来给你展示。

我已经连续运行了 5 次测试，间隔大约 1-2 秒，这就是结果

先运行 Native concat，然后运行 ​​lodash concat

测试原生连接差异
先运行 Lodash concat，然后再运行原生 concat。

测试原生连接差异
结果很明显，本机 javascript concat 获胜。 这仅使用小数据集。 使用大数据集，肯定会花费更多时间来处理。 虽然结果是亚毫秒，但时间对于让我们的应用程序运行得更快很重要。

我不是说不要使用 Lodash concat，但是嘿，如果我们已经用 javascript 内置了 concat 方法，最好使用它。 除非您有其他理由使用 lodash concat 函数并且您的应用程序并不真正关心响应时间。

它很有用，它使开发变得更容易和更快，因为我们不必实现复杂的逻辑来获得结果。 所以，这取决于你！

我们将尝试掩盖更多 lodash 和内置函数之间的差异，以便我们稍后看看！

感谢您阅读 :D Psstt pstt :p 请考虑喜欢这篇文章❤️并关注我！ 为什么不对？ 它是免费的~我真的很感激👨🏻‍💻

将发布更多与 AWS、Javascript、Python、无服务器等相关的内容！

伊曼纽尔·巴托莱西 - 一旦暂停，harithzainudin 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，harithzainudin 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，harithzainudin 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 harithzainudin 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 harithzainudin：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 harithzainudin 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Concat function in Lodash will creates a new array that concat the array with any additional arrays and/or values.
It will not change the original array.

It's easy to use and simple. Same goes to the Javascript native concat method. But what's the difference between this 2 method?

Well obviously, one is using libraries (Lodash), and another one is Javascript original concatenation.

The syntax also different. BUT, the native Javascript concat win. Let me show you.

I've run the test 5 times in a row, with about 1-2 seconds of interval and this is the result

Running Native concat first, then lodash concat

Test Native Concat Lodash Concat Differences
1 0.318ms 0.179ms ✅ 0.139ms
2 0.097ms ✅ 0.16ms -0.063ms
3 0.094ms ✅ 0.159ms -0.065ms
4 0.094ms ✅ 0.16ms -0.066ms
5 0.09ms ✅ 0.158ms -0.068ms

Running Lodash concat first and then native concat.

Test Native Concat Lodash Concat Differences
1 0.525ms 0.009ms ✅ 0.516ms
2 0.237ms 0.008ms ✅ 0.229ms
3 0.247ms 0.008ms ✅ 0.239ms
4 0.259ms 0.008ms ✅ 0.251ms
5 0.25ms 0.008ms ✅ 0.242ms

With obvious result, native javascript concat wins. This is only using small dataset. With big dataset, surely it will tak e more time to process. Although the result is sub-milliseconds, the timing is important to make our apps run faster.

I'm not saying do not use Lodash concat, but hey, if we have already concat method in built with javascript, it's better to use it. Unless you have other reason to use lodash concat function and your applications don't really care about response time.

Some of Lodash function do actually make our life easier. It is useful and it makes the development much easier and faster as we do not have to implement a complex logic to get the result. So, it is up to you!

We will try to cover up more of the differences between lodash and inbuilt function so that we can have a look later on!

Thank you for reading :D Psstt pstt :p Do consider to love this article ❤️ and follow me! Why not right? It's FREE~ I would really appreciate it 👨🏻‍💻

Will be posting more on things related to AWS, Javascript, Python, Serverless and more!

Emanuele Bartolesi - Jul 13

Items.Title: 使用 ReactJs 创建响应式导航栏。 | Create a responsive navbar with ReactJs.
Items.Description:
高塔姆古内查

发表于全视图

移动视图

 yarn create react-app navbar
  • 创建一个 Navbar 组件并将其渲染到 App.js
 import React from "react"; import Navbar from "./components/Navbar"; const App = () => { return ( <> <Navbar /> </> ); }; export default App;
  • 完整视图的 Navbar.js 代码
import React from "react"; import "./Navbar.css"; const Navbar = () => { return ( <> <nav className="navbar"> <h1>Navbar</h1> <ul> <li>Home</li> <li>Store</li> <li>About Us</li> </ul> <ul> <li>LogIn</li> <li>Register</li> </ul> </nav> </> ); }; export default Navbar;
  • 用于全视图的导航栏的 Css。
 .navbar { width: 100%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-around; padding: 1.5em; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; } .navbar h1 { cursor: pointer; font-size: 20px; } .navbar ul { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1em; } .navbar ul li { cursor: pointer; list-style-type: none; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; }

我们需要安装 3rd 方依赖才能使用汉堡图标。 为此，我们将使用 react-icons 库包。

现在要设置汉堡图标，我们必须从 react-icons 库包中导入它。

 import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi";

并显示图标

import React, { useState } from "react"; import "./Navbar.css"; import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi"; const Navbar = () => { return ( <> <nav className="navbar"> <h1>Navbar</h1> <ul> <li>Home</li> <li>Store</li> <li>About Us</li> </ul> <ul> <li>LogIn</li> <li>Register</li> </ul> <GiHamburgerMenu size={20} className="navbarIcon" /> </nav> </> ); }; export default Navbar;

有多种方法可以为这个项目创建响应式 ui，我们将使用 css 媒体查询和响应 useState 功能来设置我们的 ui 条件，并使用 jsx 我们将创建带有条件的新部分。

 import React, { useState } from "react"; import "./Navbar.css"; import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi"; const Navbar = () => { const [show, setShow] = useState(false); const handleNavbarDisplay = () => { setShow(!show); }; return ( <> <nav className="navbar"> <h1>Navbar</h1> <ul> <li>Home</li> <li>Store</li> <li>About Us</li> </ul> <ul> <li>LogIn</li> <li>Register</li> </ul> <GiHamburgerMenu onClick={() => handleNavbarDisplay()} size={20} className="navbarIcon" /> </nav> {show && ( <section className="navbarMobile"> <p>Home</p> <p>Store</p> <p>About Us</p> <p>LogIn</p> <p>Register</p> </section> )} </> ); }; export default Navbar;
 .navbar { width: 100%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-around; padding: 1.5em; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; } .navbar h1 { cursor: pointer; font-size: 20px; } .navbar ul { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1em; } .navbar ul li { cursor: pointer; list-style-type: none; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; } .navbarIcon { display: none; } /* Mobile Responsive CSS */ @media only screen and (max-width: 600px) { .navbar { justify-content: space-between; height: 70px !important; } .navbar ul { display: none; } .navbarIcon { display: block; cursor: pointer; } .navbarMobile { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1.5em; padding: 20px; justify-content: flex-start; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; } .navbarMobile p { font-size: 18px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: 600; } }

感谢您阅读本文，也分享您的观点和你们为创建响应式导航栏所采取的方法。

一旦暂停，gautamgunecha 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，gautamgunecha 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，gautamgunecha 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 gautamgunecha 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 gautamgunecha 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 gautamgunecha 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Gautam Gunecha

Posted on Jul 31

Full View

Mobile View

yarn create react-app navbar
  • Create a Navbar component and render it to App.js 
import React from "react";
import Navbar from "./components/Navbar";

const App = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <Navbar />
    </>
  );
};

export default App;
  • Code for Navbar.js For Full View 
import React from "react";
import "./Navbar.css";

const Navbar = () => {
  return (
<>
    <nav className="navbar">
      <h1>Navbar</h1>
      <ul>
        <li>Home</li>
        <li>Store</li>
        <li>About Us</li>
      </ul>

      <ul>
        <li>LogIn</li>
        <li>Register</li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
</>
  );
};

export default Navbar;
  • Css for Navbar for Full View. 
.navbar {
    width: 100%;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: space-around;
    padding: 1.5em;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc;
}

.navbar h1 {
    cursor: pointer;
    font-size: 20px;
}

.navbar ul {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 1em;
}

.navbar ul li {
    cursor: pointer;
    list-style-type: none;
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 600;
}

We need to install 3rd party dependency in order to use hamburger icon. For this we will use react-icons library pack.

Now to setup hamburger icon we have to import it from react-icons library pack.

import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi";

and to display icon

import React, { useState } from "react";
import "./Navbar.css";
import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi";

const Navbar = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <nav className="navbar">
        <h1>Navbar</h1>

        <ul>
          <li>Home</li>
          <li>Store</li>
          <li>About Us</li>
        </ul>

        <ul>
          <li>LogIn</li>
          <li>Register</li>
        </ul>

        <GiHamburgerMenu
          size={20}
          className="navbarIcon"
        />
      </nav>
    </>
  );
};

export default Navbar;

There are various approach for creating responsive ui for this project we will use css media query and react useState functionality to setup our ui condition and with jsx we will create new section with condition.

import React, { useState } from "react";
import "./Navbar.css";
import { GiHamburgerMenu } from "react-icons/gi";

const Navbar = () => {
  const [show, setShow] = useState(false);

  const handleNavbarDisplay = () => {
    setShow(!show);
  };
  return (
    <>
      <nav className="navbar">
        <h1>Navbar</h1>

        <ul>
          <li>Home</li>
          <li>Store</li>
          <li>About Us</li>
        </ul>

        <ul>
          <li>LogIn</li>
          <li>Register</li>
        </ul>

        <GiHamburgerMenu
          onClick={() => handleNavbarDisplay()}
          size={20}
          className="navbarIcon"
        />
      </nav>

      {show && (
        <section className="navbarMobile">
          <p>Home</p>
          <p>Store</p>
          <p>About Us</p>
          <p>LogIn</p>
          <p>Register</p>
        </section>
      )}
    </>
  );
};

export default Navbar;

.navbar {
    width: 100%;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: space-around;
    padding: 1.5em;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc;
}

.navbar h1 {
    cursor: pointer;
    font-size: 20px;
}

.navbar ul {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 1em;
}

.navbar ul li {
    cursor: pointer;
    list-style-type: none;
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 600;
}

.navbarIcon {
    display: none;
}

/* Mobile Responsive CSS */

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    .navbar {
        justify-content: space-between;
        height: 70px !important;
    }

    .navbar ul {
        display: none;
    }

    .navbarIcon {
        display: block;
        cursor: pointer;
    }

    .navbarMobile {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        gap: 1.5em;
        padding: 20px;
        justify-content: flex-start;
        border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc;
    }

    .navbarMobile p {
        font-size: 18px;
        cursor: pointer;
        font-weight: 600;
    }
}

Thank You for reading out this article, also share your views and approach you guys take for creating responsive navbar.

Anuvindh Sankaravilasam - Jul 31

Items.Title: chroot 安全 | chroot safety
Items.Description:
塔莱斯 L

发表于如前所述， chroot将“外部”文件系统与使用该命令启动的新进程隔离开来。

这很方便，但不安全。 有了一些创造力，这个过程就可以打破它的“chroot 监狱”。

通过文件描述符打破它

这个内核邮件列表消息中有一个简单的例子，它通过文件描述符打破它：

所以你在根。

让我们试一试：

 $ sudo debootstrap bionic customdebroot ... $ sudo chroot customdebroot/ bash # python3 >>> import os >>> fd = os.open('/', os.O_RDONLY) >>> os.mkdir('foo') >>> os.chroot('foo') >>> os.fchdir(fd) >>> >>> os.chdir('..') >>> os.chdir('..') >>> os.chdir('..') >>> os.chdir('..') >>> os.chroot('.') >>> os.mkdir('works') >>> exit() # exit $ ls -1 / bin boot dev etc home lib lib32 lib64 libx32 lost+found media mnt opt proc root run sbin snap srv swapfile sys tmp usr var works

为什么会这样？

来自维基百科：

在大多数系统上，chroot 上下文不能正确堆叠，具有足够权限的 chroot 程序可能会执行第二个 chroot 以中断。

来自 Linus Torvalds ：

是的。 字面上地。 其他一切都保持不变，包括任何打开的文件（和 cwd）。

如果你认为它是监狱，它是 chroot 中的一个“缺陷”，但它的用途远不止于此。

请注意，chroot 最常见的用法实际上并不是“监狱”类型的用法，而是构建和安装环境（即，许多包构建内容最终使用 chroot 作为创建“目标环境”的一种方式）。

更多转义选项

也值得查看他在 YouTube 上的演讲：

保护其他资源

请注意， chroot不仅不能正确隔离文件系统（如上所示），而且它也不会保护其他资源，例如挂载点和其他进程。

例如，您可以在下面看到从chroot外部启动mousepad （文本编辑器）并从其内部成功杀死：

 $ mousepad & [1] 41196 $ sudo chroot customdebroot/ bash # ps aux Error, do this: mount -t proc proc /proc # mount -t proc proc /proc # ps aux|grep mousepad 1000 41196 0.5 1.1 472356 44728 ? Sl 15:37 0:00 mousepad root 41232 0.0 0.0 11460 964 ? S+ 15:38 0:00 grep --color=auto mousepad # sudo kill 41196

用于隐藏其他进程检查命名空间和限制资源使用检查控制组 (cgroups) 。

一旦暂停，tallesl 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，tallesl 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有由 tallesl 发布的帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 tallesl 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 tallesl 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 tallesl 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Talles L

Posted on Jul 31

As seen before, chroot isolates the 'outer' filesystem from a new process started with the command.

It's handy, but not safe. With some creativity the process can break its 'chroot jail'.

Breaking it through a file descriptor

There is a simple example from this kernel mailing list message of breaking it through a file descriptor:

chroot somewhere mkdir foo fd = open / chroot foo fchdir fd chdir ".." .... chdir ".." chroot "."

so you are in root.

Let's try it out:

$ sudo debootstrap bionic customdebroot
...

$ sudo chroot customdebroot/ bash

# python3

>>> import os
>>> fd = os.open('/', os.O_RDONLY)
>>> os.mkdir('foo')
>>> os.chroot('foo')
>>> os.fchdir(fd)
>>>     
>>> os.chdir('..')
>>> os.chdir('..')
>>> os.chdir('..')
>>> os.chdir('..')
>>> os.chroot('.')
>>> os.mkdir('works')
>>> exit()

# exit

$ ls -1 /
bin
boot
dev
etc
home
lib
lib32
lib64
libx32
lost+found
media
mnt
opt
proc
root
run
sbin
snap
srv
swapfile
sys
tmp
usr
var
works

The works directory was created on the root of the actual filesystem of the machine, and this was made from a process started with chroot.

Why that happens?

From Wikipedia:

The chroot mechanism is not intended to defend against intentional tampering by privileged (root) users. On most systems, chroot contexts do not stack properly and chrooted programs with sufficient privileges may perform a second chroot to break out.

From Linus Torvalds:

> So all chroot(2) really does is reset the "/" reference?

Yes. Literally. Everything else stays the same, including any open files (and cwd).

It's a "flaw" in chroot if you consider it a jail, but it's used for so much more than that.

(...)

Note that the most common use of chroot isn't actually the "jail" kind of usage, but building and installation environments (ie a lot of package building stuff end up using chroot as a way to create the "target environment").

More escaping options

Balazs Bucsay catalogue different approaches of escaping chroot on this GitHub repository: https://github.com/earthquake/chw00t

It's worth checking his talk on YouTube as well:

Protecting other resources

Note that chroot not only doesn't properly isolate the filesystem (as demonstrated above), but it also doesn't bother with protecting other resources such as mount points and other processes.

As an example, below you can seemousepad (text editor) being started from outside chroot and been successfully killed from inside of it:

$ mousepad &
[1] 41196

$ sudo chroot customdebroot/ bash

# ps aux           
Error, do this: mount -t proc proc /proc

# mount -t proc proc /proc
# ps aux|grep mousepad
1000       41196  0.5  1.1 472356 44728 ?        Sl   15:37   0:00 mousepad
root       41232  0.0  0.0  11460   964 ?        S+   15:38   0:00 grep --color=auto mousepad

# sudo kill 41196

For hiding other process check namespaces and for limiting resource usage check control groups (cgroups).

Once suspended, tallesl will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, tallesl will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by tallesl will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If tallesl is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag tallesl:

tallesl consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging tallesl will restore default visibility to their posts.

Items.Title: JAWS DAYS 2021 演讲者体验 | JAWS DAYS 2021 Speaker Experience
Items.Description:
我叫Yasuhiro Matsuda，是JAWS-UG Kanazawa 分会的核心成员。 这是我作为 AWS 社区构建者在 dev.to 上的第一篇文章。 我是East Co, Ltd 一家名为DONGRI的字典搜索服务的基础架构工程师。

我的社交网站如下。
推特： @matyuda
领英： yasuhiro-matsuda-31945b13a
脸书： yasuhiro.matsuda.100

今天我想宣布 JAWS DAYS 2022 并解释为什么我将首次在 JAWS DAYS 2021 上发言。 我将解释为什么我第一次在 JAWS DAYS 2021 上发言。

什么是 JAWS DAYS 2022？

去年，有 3,945 名参与者注册了该活动。

金泽分会也计划设立卫星会场。
作为 JAWS-UG 秘书长，我将支持这次活动。

我是如何成为 JAWS DAYS 2021 的第一位演讲者的？

我第一次参加 JAWS DAYS 是在 2021 年 JAWS DAYS。
听说 JAWS DAYS 是一个非常大的线下活动，但不知道活动的气氛，我决定在线上活动上发言。

因为我是执行委员会的成员，所以我受到鼓舞上台。
我成为执行委员会的成员是因为当时的执行委员会主席山口先生要求我发言。
我第一次见到山口先生是在 2018 年参加 AWS 年度重大活动re:Invent时在拉斯维加斯的现场，自那以后我们再也没有在网上见过面。 我们以前从未见过面，但由于我的在线动手协作活动，我被邀请参加这次活动。

作为执行委员会的一员，因为与人的关系，才有了今天的我，作为 JAWS-UG 的一员，我觉得我能帮上一些忙，所以我决定接受执行委员会成员，也申请参加会议。

作为金泽分会的核心成员，我被邀请与同分会的加藤先生、札幌分会的小仓先生、学生分会的Sotodate女士和大分的Hirano先生一起参加会议章节。
我被邀请参加会议，会议名为“振兴JAWS-UG分会活动的提示！” 顺带一提，到现在为止，我还没有真正在线下见过小仓先生、外馆女士或平野先生。
我以主持人的身份登台，但我紧张得好像能听到自己的心跳声，尽管我看不到每个人都在网上观看。

我认为正是 JAWS-UG 小组的向心力让我们能够像经常见面的朋友一样讨论事情。 与其他会议不同，本次会议的内容完全是非技术性的，但我能够收到很多人的反馈。

我犹豫要不要发言，因为这不是一个技术话题，但我被提醒在你的输出中大胆是多么重要。

你们为什么不冒险成为演讲者呢？



原文:

My name is Yasuhiro Matsuda and I am a core member of JAWS-UG Kanazawa Chapter. This is my first post on dev.to as an AWS Community Builder. I am an infrastructure engineer for a dictionary search service called DONGRI at East Co, Ltd.

My social networking sites are as follows.
Twitter: @matyuda
LinkedIn: yasuhiro-matsuda-31945b13a
Facebook: yasuhiro.matsuda.100

Today I would like to announce JAWS DAYS 2022 and explain why I will be speaking at JAWS DAYS 2021 for the first time. I will explain why I spoke at JAWS DAYS 2021 for the first time.

What is JAWS DAYS 2022?

JAWS DAYS 2022 will be held on Oct. 8, the largest event for AWS user groups in Japan.
Last year, 3,945 participants registered for the event.

JAWS DAYS 2020 and JAWS DAYS 2021 were online events, but This year's event will be a hybrid format of online and satellite venues throughout Japan.

The Kanazawa Chapter is also planning to set up a satellite venue.
I will support this event as the JAWS-UG Secretary General.

How did I get to be the first speaker at JAWS DAYS 2021?

The first time I attended JAWS DAYS was at JAWS DAYS 2021.
I had heard that JAWS DAYS was a very big offline event, but without knowing the atmosphere of the event, I decided to speak at the online event.

I was inspired to take the stage because I was a member of the executive committee.
I became a member of the executive committee because I was asked to speak by Mr. Yamaguchi, the executive committee chair at the time.
I first met Mr. Yamaguchi on-site in Las Vegas when I attended re:Invent, a major annual AWS event, on my own dime in 2018, and we have never met online since. We had never met before, but I was asked to be a part of this event because of my online hands-on collaboration events.

The theme of JAWS DAYS 2021 was re:Connect.
As a member of the executive committee, I am where I am today because of the relationships I have made with people, and as a member of JAWS-UG, I thought I could be of some help, so I decided to accept the position of executive committee member and also apply for the session.

As a core member of the Kanazawa Chapter, I was invited to participate in the session together with Mr. Kato from the same chapter, Mr. Ogura from the Sapporo Chapter, Ms. Sotodate from the Student Chapter, and Mr. Hirano from the Oita Chapter.
I was invited to the meeting, which was called "Getting hints for revitalizing JAWS-UG chapter activities!", we went online and talked for 40 minutes about issues facing chapter activities.

Incidentally, I have never actually met Mr. Ogura, Ms. Sotodate, or Mr. Hirano offline until now.
I took the stage as a moderator, but I was so nervous that I thought I could hear my heart beating, even though I could not see everyone watching online.

I think it was the centripetal force of the JAWS-UG group that allowed us to discuss things as if we were friends that I have met often. Unlike the other sessions, the content of this session was completely non-technical, but I was able to receive feedback from many people.

I was hesitant to speak because it was not a technical topic, but I was reminded of how important it is to be bold in your output.

Why don't you all take the plunge and become speakers?
Items.Author: matyuda
